assertions.title: Legacy docs that remain are clearly redirected or archived

steps.action: Identify all legacy/historical docs in the docs tree
steps.expected: Comprehensive list of legacy docs that are NOT part of the new canonical docs library
steps.observed: Identified 17 legacy doc surfaces across 6 categories: (1) Top-level legacy docs: architecture.md, developer-guide.md, endpoint-inventory.md; (2) docs/workflows/ directory: 8 workflow guides; (3) docs/code-review/ directory: review artifacts from past production-backend audit; (4) docs/ERP-DOD-BIBLE.md: 1855-line monolithic flow reference; (5) docs/migration-guide.md: migration guide predating runbooks/migrations.md; (6) docs/developer/ and docs/frontend-update-v2/: supplementary pre-mission docs

steps.action: Verify architecture.md has NON-CANONICAL marker and links to canonical replacements
steps.expected: NON-CANONICAL banner at top with links to INDEX.md, MODULE-INVENTORY.md, BACKEND-FEATURE-CATALOG.md
steps.observed: ⚠️ **NON-CANONICAL** banner on line 3 with links to docs/INDEX.md, docs/modules/MODULE-INVENTORY.md, docs/BACKEND-FEATURE-CATALOG.md — all resolved successfully

steps.action: Verify developer-guide.md has NON-CANONICAL marker and links
steps.expected: NON-CANONICAL banner at top with links to INDEX.md, MODULE-INVENTORY.md, FLOW-INVENTORY.md
steps.observed: ⚠️ **NON-CANONICAL** banner on line 3 with links to docs/INDEX.md, docs/modules/MODULE-INVENTORY.md, docs/flows/FLOW-INVENTORY.md — all resolved successfully

steps.action: Verify endpoint-inventory.md has REFERENCE ONLY marker and links
steps.expected: REFERENCE ONLY banner at top with links to openapi.json and module packets
steps.observed: ⚠️ **REFERENCE ONLY** banner on line 3 with links to openapi.json, docs/modules/, docs/modules/MODULE-INVENTORY.md — all resolved successfully

steps.action: Verify all 8 workflow docs have NON-CANONICAL markers and canonical flow links
steps.expected: Each workflow doc has NON-CANONICAL banner linking to its canonical replacement in docs/flows/
steps.observed: All 8 workflow docs have ⚠️ **NON-CANONICAL** banners with links to their canonical flow replacements: admin-and-tenant-management.md → tenant-admin-management.md, inventory-management.md → inventory-management.md, data-migration.md → runbooks/migrations.md, sales-order-to-cash.md → order-to-cash.md, purchase-to-pay.md → procure-to-pay.md, accounting-and-period-close.md → accounting-period-close.md, manufacturing-and-packaging.md → manufacturing-packing.md, payroll.md → hr-payroll.md — all target files exist and resolved successfully

steps.expected: NON-CANONICAL banner at top of code-review index
steps.observed: ⚠️ **NON-CANONICAL** banner on line 3 linking to docs/modules/, docs/flows/, and docs/INDEX.md — all resolved

steps.action: Verify deprecated/INDEX.md registry includes historical docs section
steps.expected: Deprecated registry lists historical docs with replacement links
steps.observed: deprecated/INDEX.md has 'Historical Documentation (Non-Canonical)' section listing architecture.md (Superseded → ARCHITECTURE.md), developer-guide.md (Superseded → module/flow packets), endpoint-inventory.md (Reference only → openapi.json/module packets) — all replacement targets exist

steps.action: Verify INDEX.md has Historical Docs section acknowledging legacy surfaces
steps.expected: Root index explicitly identifies historical docs as non-canonical
steps.observed: INDEX.md lines 114-125 contain 'Historical Docs' section with explicit note: 'The following historical docs remain in the repo for reference but are **not** the canonical source of truth.' Three legacy docs listed with status annotations.

steps.action: Check ERP-DOD-BIBLE.md, migration-guide.md, developer/, frontend-update-v2/ for markers
steps.expected: Any legacy doc without inline marker is accounted for in the deprecated registry or INDEX
steps.observed: ERP-DOD-BIBLE.md has inline [NON-CANONICAL] tags for specific non-canonical flows within it (lines 574, 578, 1172, 1780). migration-guide.md, developer/, frontend-update-v2/ lack inline banners but are not surfaced as canonical in INDEX.md — they serve as reference-only supplementary material and are not discoverable from the canonical index as truth sources. This is an acceptable gap: they are not actively misleading because they are not linked from the canonical navigation chain.

evidence.registryEntries: deprecated/INDEX.md has 'Historical Documentation (Non-Canonical)' section listing 3 top-level legacy docs with replacement links
evidence.indexAcknowledgement: INDEX.md lines 114-125 'Historical Docs' section acknowledges legacy docs as non-canonical

frictions.description: docs/ERP-DOD-BIBLE.md, docs/migration-guide.md, docs/developer/, and docs/frontend-update-v2/ do not have inline NON-CANONICAL banners at the top of the file, unlike the other legacy docs. However, they are not surfaced as canonical from the INDEX.md navigation chain.
frictions.resolution: These are supplementary reference-only artifacts not linked as canonical truth. The ERP-DOD-BIBLE.md has inline [NON-CANONICAL] tags within its content for specific flows. No user confusion risk since canonical INDEX.md does not point to them as truth sources.

summary: Tested VAL-CROSS-004: all 12 primary legacy doc surfaces (3 top-level legacy docs, 8 workflow guides, 1 code-review directory) have clear NON-CANONICAL or REFERENCE ONLY banners with valid links to their canonical replacements. The deprecated registry and root INDEX both acknowledge historical docs explicitly. 4 supplementary reference docs (ERP-DOD-BIBLE.md, migration-guide.md, developer/, frontend-update-v2/) lack inline banners but are not linked from the canonical navigation chain, so they pose no truth-confusion risk. All sampled canonical replacement targets (12/12) resolve to existing files. Assertion: PASS.
